previous_submit_handling: Before this mission, the /apply form was a plain static HTML <form> with no action, no method, and no JavaScript onSubmit handler. Browser default submit issued a same-page GET navigation that put field values into the URL query string (e.g. /apply?fullName=…&email=…). No server handler, third-party service, localStorage, or database received the submission — data was not durably stored anywhere.
changed_to: The form now POSTs JSON to the server-side endpoint POST /api/apply on https://www.vygo.ai. The edge function validates full_name and work_email, then inserts one row into the Railway Postgres applications table (directly when DATABASE_URL is present on the edge, otherwise via server-to-server proxy to the Railway API). The API responds with the created row (id, full_name, work_email, product_url, message, source, created_at). GET /api/apply/<id> reads the same row back. The client never writes to the database.

topology.marketing: Vercel (www.vygo.ai TLS + static export + edge functions)
